reasoning: The Bank Term Funding Program (BTFP) was an emergency lending facility created on March 12, 2023. It ceased making new loans on March 11, 2024. Since all loans under the BTFP had a maximum term of one year, the final loans naturally matured on March 11, 2025. This matches the FRED data, which shows the outstanding balance appropriately dropped to 0.0 on March 12, 2025, and has remained exactly 0.0 since. With the program officially closed, no new loans can be issued. The balance is therefore extremely likely to remain at 0.0 for the foreseeable future, barring an unprecedented reopening of the specific facility due to a new banking crisis (which is unlikely given the Fed prefers standard Discount Window operations and the Standing Repo Facility). Given the hard closure, the probability of an increase above 0.0 is at the minimum allowed limit (0.02) for near-term dates, and minimally higher (0.03) for later dates to account for the marginal possibility of a severe systemic event leading to the literal reinstatement of the BTFP name.
